positions.name: iShares Core S&P 500 UCITS ETF (Acc)
positions.thesis: Core broad market exposure to S&P 500. IE-domiciled accumulating structure eliminates 30% WHT on distributions — structurally optimal vehicle for a Lebanese resident. Portfolio anchor targeting ~36% of NAV. MILESTONE (April 20, 2026): The long-deferred $250 ADD was finally executed at April 20 open (confirmed by position growth from 1.4745 to 1.8025 shares, avg cost now $717.48, current price $762.96 = +6.34% unrealized). Weight improved from 26.2% to 33.96% of total portfolio NAV. The 9-report operational failure is now resolved. Remaining gap to 36% target: approximately $100-110. ASML exit proceeds and remaining cash will be used to close this gap by May 3 hard deadline. S&P 500 near all-time highs (~7,124 on April 21). No structural invalidation conditions triggered.
invalidation_conditions: Structural shift away from US equities as the dominant long-term growth engine
A superior WHT-efficient broad market vehicle becomes available

positions.name: Amazon.com Inc
positions.thesis: Conviction dip buy entered during Liberation Day tariff shock. Core thesis: AWS cloud dominance (~33% global cloud market share), advertising revenue at double-digit growth, and AI infrastructure investment (Bedrock, Trainium custom silicon). AWS Q4 2025: +24% YoY revenue growth (fastest in 13 quarters), $15B AI revenue run rate. $200B 2026 capex commitment. Q1 2026 earnings confirmed April 29, 2026 at 5:30 PM ET. No dividend = 0% WHT. As of April 20: up +16.05% from entry ($248.28 vs cost $213.94). TD Cowen Buy at $300 price target (+21% upside from current). Q1 2026 operating income guidance range ($16.5-21.5B) was wide and the low end disappointed the market — watch for execution. RSI elevated pre-earnings; no pre-earnings adds. Post-earnings beat (AWS >20% growth AND operating income above low end of guidance) is the sizing review trigger.
invalidation_conditions: AWS loses meaningful cloud market share to Azure or GCP on a sustained basis
Regulatory breakup that separates AWS from the retail/advertising business
Sustained margin compression across all segments (retail, advertising, AND cloud simultaneously)

positions.name: SPDR Gold Shares
positions.thesis: Macro hedge against dollar weakness, geopolitical risk, and inflationary pressure. Gold has no cash flows so carries zero WHT exposure despite US domicile. APRIL 21 UPDATE: Iran re-closed the Strait of Hormuz on April 18-19, citing US 'breaches of trust' — reversing the brief April 17 reopening. The fragile ceasefire expires Wednesday April 23. Tehran is vowing retaliation after a US vessel seizure. The IEA described this as 'the largest oil supply disruption in the history of the global oil market.' The 4-week sustained risk-on clock for GLD invalidation has NOT started and remains fully reset. GLD at $442.09 (April 20 close) — 5.3% above the $420 invalidation trigger. XAU near $4,790. Institutional targets: Goldman Sachs end-2026 $5,400/oz; JPMorgan $4,000-$6,300; UBS $5,600/oz. Central bank purchases on track for 755 tonnes in 2026 (well above pre-2022 averages). Thesis conviction: STRONGER than prior report given accelerating Iran escalation.
invalidation_conditions: Sustained USD strengthening cycle driven by structurally higher real rates that persist above inflation
Major reversal in central bank gold buying trend (multiple major central banks becoming net sellers)
GLD breaks and holds below $420 — triggers 25% trim rebalance into CSPX

Iran war fully and durably resolved AND Strait of Hormuz formally reopened AND risk-on environment maintained for 4+ consecutive weeks — assess 25% trim at that point (4-week clock fully reset as of April 18-19 Hormuz re-closure)

positions.name: Mastercard Inc
positions.wht_exposure: 30% WHT on dividend — confirmed $0.22 withheld on $0.73 April dividend. Annualised WHT drag <$1 at current position size. Immaterial.
positions.thesis: Conviction dip buy during Liberation Day volatility. Thesis: payments network duopoly (MA + Visa) with near-monopoly economics, asset-light model generating high ROE, global consumer spending volume exposure, and pricing power. As of April 20: up +0.66% unrealized ($516.65 vs cost $513.24). 35/38 analysts Buy/Outperform; $654.66 average price target (+26% upside from current). EARNINGS DATE CONFIRMED: Q1 2026 earnings report April 30, 2026. Q1 2026 guidance: revenue growth at low end of low-double digits on a currency-neutral basis. Key watch metrics for ADD $100 decision: cross-border volume must exceed Q4 2025's 14% rate, and VAS growth must sustain above 20% (Q4 exit rate 22%). Q1 data covers January-March 2026 (largely pre-Iran escalation), so volumes should be clean. Stagflation risk from Iran/Hormuz is a medium-term headwind to cross-border volumes but unlikely to show in Q1. Dividend yield <1% means WHT drag is under $1/year at current size.
invalidation_conditions: Disintermediation by real-time payment rails (FedNow, open banking) gaining scale at the expense of card networks
Regulatory caps on interchange fees in core US or European markets
Q1 2026 earnings miss on volumes or management guides down — re-evaluate immediately on April 30
Sustained global consumer spending contraction lasting multiple quarters

positions.name: NVIDIA Corporation
positions.thesis: AI infrastructure buildout driving unprecedented GPU demand. NVDA holds dominant position in AI training and inference hardware; CUDA ecosystem creates deep switching costs. As of April 20: up +11.04% from entry ($202.06 vs cost $181.98). Blackwell systems sold out through mid-2026 at ~$40,000 per GPU. $1 trillion in confirmed AI chip demand through 2027 — actual purchase orders, not speculative projections. Hyperscalers (Microsoft, Amazon, Google, Meta) committed $200B+ in combined AI infrastructure spend in 2026. Vera Rubin platform (H2 2026 production, HBM4, 3nm process) is the next major re-rating catalyst. 38 analysts Strong Buy; $264-274 consensus price target (+31-35% upside). Q1 FY2027 earnings approximately May 20, 2026. Do not add at elevated levels; reassess on $180-185 pullback. Thesis conviction: STRONGEST IN PORTFOLIO.
invalidation_conditions: AMD or custom silicon (Google TPU, Amazon Trainium, Microsoft Maia) takes meaningful and sustained share in AI training workloads
AI capex cycle reverses — hyperscalers visibly cut or guide down infrastructure spend across multiple quarters
Export restrictions to China expand materially beyond current guidance, reducing total addressable market by >15%
Post-GTC 2026: $1T order backlog proves overstated or a significant portion of orders cancelled in subsequent quarters

positions.name: ASML Holding NV
positions.wht_exposure: 15% Dutch WHT assumed (treaty rate). Verification still outstanding — check next IBKR tax document. NOW MOOT IF EXIT IS EXECUTED AS RECOMMENDED.
positions.thesis: THESIS FLAG: DETERIORATING — EXIT RECOMMENDED AS OF 2026-04-21. EUV lithography monopoly remains structurally intact (no credible competitor on a 5-10 year horizon, every advanced fab ASML-dependent). However, the invalidation conditions are materializing faster than expected: (1) China mix collapsed from 36% (Q4 2025) to 19% (Q1 2026) in a single quarter — 17pp compression — and is on a clear trajectory to breach the 15% invalidation threshold in Q2 2026 results (to be released approximately July 2026). (2) NEW: A fresh China export threat emerged April 20, 2026 (beyond what was already known), introducing additional restriction risk. (3) Stock fell 6% post-Q1 results despite a beat (EUR 8.8B revenue vs EUR 8.5B consensus) and a full-year guidance raise to EUR 36-40B — the market is pre-pricing the multiple de-rating that Jefferies explicitly forecasted. As of April 20: +5.68% unrealized ($1,476.50 vs cost $1,397.14). Full exit recommended — redeploy into CSPX and reserve for MA April 30.
invalidation_conditions: China develops a credible domestic EUV alternative — assessed as a 5-10 year horizon risk, not near-term
Semiconductor capex supercycle ends and fab customers cancel or significantly delay EUV orders at scale
Dutch or US government export restrictions expand materially beyond current ASML guidance, causing revenue guidance to be CUT (not merely compressed China mix)
China mix falls below 15% of net sales in Q2 2026 results — APPROACHING THRESHOLD: currently at 19% and declining rapidly
US DUV restriction bill passes into law — would directly cut remaining China revenue and trigger immediate exit and redeploy into CSPX/NVDA
NEW (April 20, 2026): Additional China export restriction threat beyond existing controls — materializing

positions.name: iShares USD Treasury Bond 0-3 Month UCITS ETF (Acc)
positions.thesis: Cash equivalent / capital preservation layer with 0% effective WHT. Replaced SGOV (which carried 30% WHT on distributions) — structurally optimal for Lebanese residents. US Treasury interest paid to an Irish-domiciled fund qualifies for the portfolio interest exemption from NRA withholding. Near-zero duration risk (0-3M). YTM at ~3.68% as of April 13, 2026 — above the 3.0% trim trigger but below the ~4.3% thesis baseline. At 14.83% of total portfolio NAV. JPMorgan forecasts no Fed cuts in 2026 (possible 25bps hike Q3 2027), meaning yields should remain stable at current levels. Iran re-closure of Hormuz on April 18-19 partially reinforces near-term capital preservation rationale. STAGE-DOWN PLAN ACTIVE: Target reduction from ~14.83% toward ~10% of NAV in stages. April 28-29 FOMC is the next trigger: dovish signal = first stage-down of ~$100 redeployed into CSPX. Do not trim U03A before FOMC clarity.
invalidation_conditions: Fed cuts rates aggressively (>100bps in 2026) — opportunity cost vs equities rises significantly, accelerate trim
A better WHT-efficient fixed income vehicle with superior risk-adjusted return is identified
Portfolio NAV grows large enough (>$10K) that maintaining a 15% cash equivalent layer imposes unacceptable drag on long-term returns
YTM falls below 3.0% — at that point, reduce toward 8-10% of NAV immediately
